summary: Converged the private Gitea runtime on docker-runtime-lv3 with PostgreSQL and Keycloak integration, published the host-side Tailscale proxy on 100.64.0.1:3009, registered the docker-build-lv3 Actions runner, and verified a push-triggered workflow end to end with a temporary smoke repository under the ops organization.

verification.observed: `make converge-gitea` exited 0 after converging the Proxmox host proxy, the postgres-lv3 database role, the docker-runtime-lv3 Gitea stack, and the docker-build-lv3 Actions runner.

The Proxmox host returned `{"version":"1.25.5"}` from `http://100.64.0.1:3009/api/v1/version`, and both `/api/swagger` and `/user/login` answered successfully through the host proxy and on docker-runtime-lv3 at `http://10.10.10.20:3003`.

verification.check: Runner registration and host container state
verification.observed: The live admin API reported one online runner named `docker-build-lv3`, and `docker ps --filter name=lv3-gitea-runner` on docker-build-lv3 showed `lv3-gitea-runner Up 2 hours`.

verification.check: Repository creation and push-triggered workflow smoke
verification.observed: Using the mirrored admin token through an SSH tunnel to the host proxy, the temporary repo `ops/ci-webhook-smoke-20260326121130` was created, a workflow was seeded, a push-trigger commit `43a4237b8370093fc67816a9b339bbaf6cf567c6` was recorded, and Actions run `4` completed with `status=completed` and `conclusion=success` before the smoke repo was deleted.

notes: The controller shell still could not route directly to the host-side Tailscale proxy without an SSH tunnel, even though the Proxmox host itself answered the same controller URL and the repo automation converge succeeded end to end.
The temporary smoke repository was deleted after verification to avoid leaving disposable CI fixtures in the production Gitea org.
